I am not getting any errors, but the code is not finding the cell value that does not match the sequence
Apologies for the confusion. To make sure the code finds the cell value that does not match the sequence, we need to modify the logic for finding the next non-empty cell.

Please use the updated code below:

Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets(“Sheet1”) ’ Replace “Sheet1” with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range(“B3”) ’ Replace “B3” with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = ws.Range(startCell, startCell.End(xlDown)).Find(“*”, LookIn:=xlValues, SearchDirection:=xlNext)
    
    Dim currentDay As Variant
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    ’ Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ’ Try finding the next non-empty cell below
        On Error Resume Next
        Set currentCell = ws.Range(currentCell.Offset(1), ws.Cells(1000, currentCell.Column)).SpecialCells(xlCellTypeConstants).Cells(1)
        On Error GoTo 0
        If currentCell Is Nothing Then Exit Sub ’ If reached an empty cell, exit the loop

        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array(“Monday”, “Tuesday”, “Wednesday”, “Thursday”, “Friday”, “Saturday”, “Sunday”), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array(“Monday”, “Tuesday”, “Wednesday”, “Thursday”, “Friday”, “Saturday”, “Sunday”), 0) Mod 7) + 1)

I apologize, there seems to be some syntax errors in the provided code.

Here's the updated code:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Sheet1") ' Replace "Sheet1" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    
    Dim currentDay As Variant
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    ' Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Try finding the next non-empty cell below
        Set currentCell = currentCell.Offset(1)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Do ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
        
        If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
            ' Highlight the cell if it does not follow the sequence
            currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 0, 0)
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```

Please replace "Sheet1" with the name of your worksheet and "B3" with the actual starting cell address in Column B. This code should highlight any cell in Column B that does not follow the sequence of days ("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday").

A 400 error typically indicates a syntax error or an invalid object or property. In the updated code I provided, there was a syntax error in the line that highlights the cell with a non-matching value.

Please replace the line:

```vba
currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 0, 0)
```

with:

```vba
currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
```

This will set the cell's interior color to red.

If you're still encountering a 400 error, please provide more specific details about the error message and highlight the line that is causing the error.
I am still getting the 400 error but my VBA editor is not highlighting the line that is causing it

I apologize for the confusion. To debug the 400 error, we can use error handling to display the specific error message and the line causing the error.

Please replace the code inside the loop with the updated code below:

```vba
    On Error Resume Next
    If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
        currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
        If Err.Number <> 0 Then
            MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description & vbNewLine & "Line: " & Erl
            Exit Sub
        End If
    End If
    On Error GoTo 0
```

This code will display a message box with the specific error message and the line causing the error.

Please try running the code again and let me know the error message and the line causing the error.

The "Application-defined or object-defined error" typically occurs when there is an issue with referencing an object or property correctly.

```vba

If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then

    If Err.Number <> 0 Then
        MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description
        Exit Sub
    End If
End If
On Error GoTo 0
```

This code will display a message box with the specific error description.

Please try running the code again and let me know the error message you receive. Additionally, please let me know the line at which the error occurs.

I apologize for the confusion. Let's modify the code to handle the error and display the specific error message. Replace the code inside the loop with the following:

```vba
On Error Resume Next

nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description
    Exit Sub
End If

If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
    currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
End If

On Error GoTo 0
```


This code will display a message box with the specific error description if an error occurs at the line calculating `nextDay`.

Please try running the code again and let me know the error message you receive.
Error: Unable to get the Match property of the WorksheetFunction class

The "Unable to get the Match property of the WorksheetFunction class" error typically occurs when there is no match found using the `Match` function.

To handle this error and provide a more informative message, we can modify the code as follows:

```vba
On Error Resume Next

nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description
    Exit Sub
End If

On Error GoTo 0

If IsError(nextDay) Then
    MsgBox "Error: Could not find a matching day for " & currentDay
    Exit Sub
End If

If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
    currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
End If
```

This code adds an additional error handling block to check if `nextDay` is an error value. It displays a message box with the specific error description if an error occurred during the calculation. It also checks if `nextDay` has an error value, indicating that there is no match found for the current day, and displays a message box in that case.

Please try running the code again and let me know the error message you receive.

I am getting an error 'Type mismatch' with this code: Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("PORTAL") ' Replace "Sheet1" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    
    Dim currentDay As String
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As String
    
    ' Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Try finding the next non-empty cell below
        Set currentCell = currentCell.Offset(1)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Do ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        
On Error Resume Next

nextDay = Application.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0)
nextDay = CStr(nextDay)
nextDay = Val(nextDay) Mod 7 + 1
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    MsgBox "Error: " & Err.Description
    Exit Sub
End If

If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
    currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
End If

On Error GoTo 0
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub

I apologize for the mistake. The issue is with the assignment of `nextDay` variable. We should assign the corresponding day of the week, not the index. Also, we don't need to convert `nextDay` to a string and then back to a number. 

Please replace the portion of code inside the loop with the updated code below:

```vba
' Check if the value follows the sequence
On Error Resume Next
nextDay = Application.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0)
On Error GoTo 0

If IsError(nextDay) Then
    MsgBox "Error: Could not find a matching day for " & currentDay
    Exit Sub
End If

Select Case nextDay ' Assign the corresponding day of the week
    Case 1
        nextDay = "Monday"
    Case 2
        nextDay = "Tuesday"
    Case 3
        nextDay = "Wednesday"
    Case 4
        nextDay = "Thursday"
    Case 5
        nextDay = "Friday"
    Case 6
        nextDay = "Saturday"
    Case 7
        nextDay = "Sunday"
End Select

If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
    currentCell.Interior.Color = vbRed
End If
```

This code fixes the type mismatch error and assigns the corresponding day of the week to the `nextDay` variable based on the index.

Apologies for the confusion. The issue lies in the comparison of the cell value with the `nextDay` variable.

Please replace the line:

```vba
If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
```

with:

```vba

```

This will convert the `nextDay` variable to a string before comparing it with the cell value.

Here's the updated code:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("PORTAL") ' Replace "PORTAL" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    
    Dim currentDay As String
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As String
    
    ' Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Try finding the next non-empty cell below
        Set currentCell = currentCell.Offset(1)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Do ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        On Error Resume Next
        nextDay = Application.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0)
        On Error GoTo 0
    
        If IsError(nextDay) Then
            MsgBox "Error: Could not find a matching day for " & currentDay
            Exit Sub
        End If
    
        Select Case nextDay ' Assign the corresponding day of the week
            Case 1
                nextDay = "Monday"
            Case 2
                nextDay = "Tuesday"
            Case 3
                nextDay = "Wednesday"
            Case 4
                nextDay = "Thursday"
            Case 5
                nextDay = "Friday"
            Case 6
                nextDay = "Saturday"
            Case 7
                nextDay = "Sunday"
        End Select
    
        If currentCell.Value <> CStr(nextDay) Then
            currentCell.Interior.Color = vbYellow
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```

Please try running the code again, and it should highlight only the specific cell that is not in sequence.
It is still highlighting the entire range and not the specific cell out of sequence

Apologies for the confusion. It seems that I overlooked an important detail in the code.

Please replace the line:

```vba
If currentCell.Value <> CStr(nextDay) Then
```

with:

```vba
If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
```

And replace the line inside the `Do Until` loop that sets the interior color:

```vba
currentCell.Interior.Color = vbYellow
```

with:

```vba
currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Change to your desired highlight color
```

Here's the updated code:

```vba
Sub HighlightSequenceDisruption()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("PORTAL") ' Replace "PORTAL" with your actual sheet name
    
    Dim startCell As Range
    Set startCell = ws.Range("B3") ' Replace "B3" with the starting cell address in Column B
    
    Dim currentCell As Range
    Set currentCell = startCell
    
    Dim currentDay As String
    currentDay = currentCell.Value
    
    Dim nextDay As Variant
    
    ' Loop through the range from the first non-empty cell to B1000
    Do Until currentCell.Row > 1000
        ' Try finding the next non-empty cell below
        Set currentCell = currentCell.Offset(1)
        If IsEmpty(currentCell) Then Exit Do ' If reached an empty cell, exit the loop
        
        ' Check if the value follows the sequence
        nextDay = WorksheetFunction.Index(Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), (WorksheetFunction.Match(currentDay, Array("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"), 0) Mod 7) + 1)
        
        If currentCell.Value <> nextDay Then
            ' Highlight the specific cell that is not in sequence
            currentCell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Change to your desired highlight color
        End If
        
        currentDay = currentCell.Value
    Loop
End Sub
```
